seoTitle: How to Use Jira Automation Without Code
metaDescription: Learn how to build Jira automation rules using triggers, conditions, and actions to automate issue updates, assignments, and notifications.
intro: Repetitive tasks like updating statuses or notifying assignees eat into time better spent on real work. This guide covers building Jira automation rules using triggers, conditions, and actions, without needing to write any code.

steps.heading: Open Jira Automation
steps.body: Go to your project settings and navigate to the Automation section to access the rule builder.

steps.heading: Create an automation rule
steps.body: Click Create Rule to start building a new automation from scratch.

steps.heading: Add triggers, conditions, and actions
steps.body: Choose a trigger like an issue transition, add optional conditions to narrow the scope, and set an action such as updating a field or sending a notification.

steps.heading: Test and enable the automation
steps.body: Run a test to confirm the rule behaves as expected, then turn it on so it runs automatically going forward.

faq.question: Do I need to know how to code to use Jira Automation?
faq.answer: No, Jira Automation is a no-code rule builder that lets you configure triggers, conditions, and actions through a visual interface.

faq.question: What can Jira Automation do?
faq.answer: It can automate issue updates and assignments, send notifications, and handle other repetitive tasks based on rules you configure.

faq.question: How do I test an automation rule before turning it on?
faq.answer: Most automation builders let you run a test execution of the rule so you can confirm it behaves correctly before enabling it.

faq.question: Can I restrict an automation rule to specific issues?
faq.answer: Yes, adding conditions to your rule lets you narrow it down to only the issues that match specific criteria.
